cards.title: OpenAI Expands ChatGPT for Families and Caregivers
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build ChatGPT experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults. The move signals the company's strategy to deepen household penetration beyond individual users.

cards.title: Meta Removes Controversial Instagram AI Feature
cards.body: Meta has discontinued a feature that referenced public Instagram content for AI image generation following user backlash. The company acknowledged the feature "missed the mark" despite intentions to provide creative tools.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secret Theft
cards.body: Apple alleges OpenAI misappropriated trade secrets, with the misconduct directed by senior leadership including a former Apple employee. The lawsuit intensifies tensions between tech giants competing in AI development.

cards.title: Open Source AI Dominates Market Growth
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue reports open source AI is booming, with roughly half the Fortune 500 now using the platform's shared models and datasets. The company has become the GitHub of AI development.

cards.title: SK Hynix Raises $26.5B in Record IPO
cards.body: SK Hynix completed the largest foreign IPO in U.S. history, fueled by the AI chip boom. Policymakers are now urging the company and Samsung to build new manufacturing facilities in the U.S.

cards.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Chosen for Microsoft Copilot
cards.body: OpenAI announced its new GPT-5.6 family will continue powering Microsoft's Copilot 365 productivity suite. The partnership deepens amid speculation about OpenAI's potential independence and IPO plans.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Fidji Simo Departs
cards.body: Fidji Simo is stepping down from her full-time role as OpenAI's No. 2 after extended medical leave. The departure creates a leadership vacuum as the company pursues an IPO and competes with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI released its latest GPT-5.6 family of models with improvements across cybersecurity and other capabilities. The new models continue the company's aggressive product release schedule.

cards.title: AI Agent Startup Raises $100M Using Own AI
cards.body: Lyzr used its own AI agent to autonomously manage a $100 million fundraising round, demonstrating real-world enterprise capability. The move serves as proof-of-concept for AI agent reliability in business-critical tasks.

cards.title: OpenAI Shuts Down Atlas Browser
cards.body: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered Atlas browser after less than a year of operation. The company is redirecting agentic browsing features to its desktop app and Chrome extension instead.

cards.title: Elon Musk Backs Anthropic Infrastructure Plans
cards.body: Elon Musk pledged not to "cut off" Anthropic while hosting its models, promising reliable infrastructure for the $40 billion revenue company. The commitment addresses trust concerns about hosting with Musk's xAI infrastructure.

cards.title: AI ROI Debate Intensifies with $3 Trillion Stakes
cards.body: The return of the AI ROI conversation carries increasingly high stakes, with some estimates reaching $3 trillion in potential value. Enterprises face mounting pressure to demonstrate concrete returns on AI investments.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark 1.1 for Coding
cards.body: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, an AI coding assistant designed for large agentic workloads, bug fixes, and code migrations. The tool targets enterprise customers increasingly seeking AI-driven automation.

cards.title: New York Times Claims OpenAI Hid Evidence
cards.body: The New York Times alleges OpenAI concealed tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT training. Publishers escalated their lawsuit with a motion seeking sanctions against OpenAI.

cards.title: Google Requires Disclosure of AI-Generated Ads
cards.body: Google now mandates disclosure when ads contain AI-generated or synthetic content, expanding requirements previously limited to political ads. The policy aims to increase transparency while permitting AI-assisted creative tools.

cards.title: Gradium Raises $100M Seed Backed by Nvidia
cards.body: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium secured $100 million in seed funding with Nvidia backing, signaling confidence in voice AI technology. The company plans to open a Bay Area office to compete for top talent.

cards.title: Government Approval Process for Frontier AI Unclear
cards.body: Details remain murky about how the government approved OpenAI's frontier model release for public use. The exact dialog between regulators and major AI labs has not been publicly disclosed.

cards.title: Instagram Users Can Opt Out of Meta AI
cards.body: Meta's Muse Image feature allows AI generation using photos from public Instagram accounts, but users can now disable this. The feature had raised privacy concerns despite Meta's intent to provide creative tools.

cards.title: Meta's Custom AI Chips Begin Production
cards.body: Meta's custom-designed AI chips will enter production in September with a modular architecture to accommodate rapid AI evolution. The approach allows the company to adapt chip designs as AI capabilities advance.

cards.title: OpenAI Targets Families With ChatGPT
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a product manager to build ChatGPT experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults. The move signals the company's push to deepen household adoption of its AI assistant.

cards.title: Meta Kills Controversial AI Instagram Feature
cards.body: Meta removed an AI feature that generated images using public Instagram photos after user backlash. The company acknowledged the feature missed the mark and is no longer available.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple alleges OpenAI stole trade secrets, with misconduct allegedly directed by senior leadership. The lawsuit involves a longtime former employee at the center of the claims.

cards.title: Open Source AI Booming, Says Hugging Face
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue reports open source AI is thriving as the platform grows into a GitHub-like hub for AI models. The platform is now used by roughly half the Fortune 500.

cards.title: OpenAI's GPT 5.6 Powers Microsoft Copilot
cards.body: OpenAI's new model family will continue powering Microsoft's workplace productivity apps like Copilot 365. The partnership remains strong amid speculation about the companies' future relationship.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Steps Down
cards.body: Fidji Simo is stepping down from her full-time role as OpenAI's No. 2 after extended medical leave. The departure creates a leadership gap as OpenAI eyes an IPO and competes with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI released its new family of models with improvements across cybersecurity and other domains. The launch represents the company's latest push in AI capability advancement.

cards.title: AI Agent Startup Raises $100M Using AI
cards.body: Lyzr used its own AI agent to lead a $100 million fundraising round, demonstrating the technology's enterprise viability. The move serves as proof the product delivers real-world value.

cards.title: OpenAI Shuts Down Atlas Browser
cards.body: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. The company is moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and Chrome extension instead.

cards.title: Meta Launches AI Coding Tool Muse Spark
cards.body: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1 to compete in the crowded AI coding space with capabilities for large workloads and bug fixes. The tool targets enterprise automation needs.

cards.title: New York Times: OpenAI Hid Copyright Evidence
cards.body: News publishers claim OpenAI concealed tools and datasets in the ChatGPT copyright lawsuit. The allegation escalates litigation with a new motion for sanctions.

cards.title: Google Requires AI Disclosure in Ads
cards.body: Google will now mandate disclosure when ads use AI to create synthetic or digitally altered content. Previously, only election ads required such transparency.

cards.title: AI Voice Startup Gradium Raises $100M
cards.body: Paris-based Gradium secured $100 million in seed funding backed by Nvidia. The company plans to open a Bay Area office to compete for AI talent.

cards.title: Meta's AI Chips Enter Production September
cards.body: Meta is taking a modular approach to chip design to accommodate rapid AI evolution. Production of the company's new AI chips begins in September.

cards.title: Anthropic Peers Inside Claude's Reasoning
cards.body: Anthropic developed a technique revealing hidden spaces where Claude processes concepts before responding. The discovery offers unprecedented insight into how large language models function.

cards.title: Sam Altman's AI Wealth-Sharing Proposal Returns
cards.body: Sam Altman's idea that Americans should share in AI-generated wealth is back in the spotlight. Reports suggest individuals could have a $300 stake in OpenAI's success.

cards.title: IT Leaders Scale AI Architecture Strategy
cards.body: Organizations are expanding AI use cases and moving toward agentic systems as capabilities advance. IT leaders face uncertainty about which investments will remain valuable amid rapid evolution.
